questions: Walk me through a typical week for you right now — where does most of your time actually go?
What's something you find yourself doing over and over across projects — the kind of thing you've done a hundred times before?
When a new junior person joins, what's the first thing you train them on so it's off your plate?
Tell me about the last time you needed to track down a code or spec detail mid-design — what did that actually look like?
Walk me through what happens when a drawing or spec is ready for review — who touches it, and where does it usually get stuck?
Think about the last RFI you dealt with — what kicked it off, and how much back-and-forth did it take to close out?
If you had an expert on call 24/7 who'd read every code, spec, and past project you've touched, what's the first thing you'd ask them?
If you got five hours back a week, what would you actually do with them?
